query: My MCP server needs to call the GitHub REST API to fetch issue and PR data. The auth is a personal access token. How do I design the egress and secret handling so it'll pass an auditor's review?
expected_behavior: Recommends documenting the exact egress target (api.github.com) explicitly in both the README and an auditable manifest field so consumers can verify before install

Recommends consuming the token via an environment variable (not a hardcoded value, not a config file checked into the repo) and never logging or echoing the token in any code path, including error messages
Recommends requesting the least-privilege OAuth scope or fine-grained personal access token (e.g., read-only repo + issues scopes) rather than a full-access classic PAT
